claim.content: The use of the empirical observation that hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) substrates can induce single-particle energy gaps as large as ~30 meV in monolayer graphene to argue that alignment to hBN can substantially modify the energetic competition among correlated many-body ground states in flat-band twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG) assumes that the single-particle sublattice-symmetry-breaking potential effects of hBN alignment on monolayer graphene are at least qualitatively transferable to the correlated, narrow-bandwidth, multi-band moiré electronic structure of tBLG; this extrapolation presumes that comparable gap scales and their influence can persist in the moiré flat-band environment. Symbols: "hBN" denotes hexagonal boron nitride, "meV" denotes milli–electron-volt, and tBLG denotes twisted bilayer graphene.
